_meta.description: Microsoft Word style definitions for formatting Canadian Science Advisory Secretariat (CSAS) publications. A formatting tool reads this file to convert a plain Word document into CSAS format by creating/updating each named style with the properties below.
_meta.derived_from: data/2026_Templates/ - 10 documents (5 publication types x English/French).
_meta.units: All sizes in points (pt). 1 inch = 72 pt; 1 cm ~= 28.35 pt. Colours are 6-digit hex RRGGBB, or 'automatic' (theme/black).

formatting_options.csas_publication: Apply one of the templates under 'templates' below, chosen by publication type.
formatting_options.no_change: Leave the source document's formatting untouched (no spec needed).

conventions.based_on: Word style this style inherits from.
conventions.used_in: How many source documents applied this style.
conventions.source_variations: Present when source documents disagreed; lists the values seen. The value given is the majority/normalised one.
conventions.borders: Optional, inside 'paragraph'. Border edges to apply; a style without it has any borders cleared. Widths and spacing in points.
conventions.also_named: Other styleIds the same style appears under in the source documents (the JSON key is the canonical English styleId). Mostly French-language styleIds; a conversion tool should treat any of these as the same style.
conventions.linked_character_style: Word auto-creates this linked character style; it mirrors the paragraph style's run formatting.

document_defaults.note: The effective default comes from the Normal style. Word's raw docDefaults list Times New Roman, but Normal overrides it to Arial 11 pt.

Normal.purpose: Base paragraph style. All other paragraph styles inherit from it; carries the document default font.

BodyText.purpose: Default body paragraph for running text.

BodyTextCentered.purpose: Body paragraph, centred. Some French templates name this 'BodyTextCentr'.

CoverPageHeaderCSAS.purpose: Cover page CSAS series header line.

PageHeaderRegionsNameofthereport.style_name: Page Header: Region(s) + Name of the report
PageHeaderRegionsNameofthereport.purpose: Running page header (region(s) and report name).

PageNumber.purpose: Page-number field text in the footer.

advisory_response_report.list_marker_note: List markers are defined in the document's numbering part, not the style: ListBullet = solid bullet, ListBullet2 = hollow 'o', ListNumber = '1.', ListNumber2 = 'a.'. Level-1 lists indent the text ~18 pt with an 18 pt hanging indent; level-2 lists ~32-36 pt.

document_defaults.note: Unlike the advisory/response template, the Research Document's Normal style itself carries 6 pt space before/after.

Normal.purpose: Base paragraph style. All other paragraph styles inherit from it.

BodyText.purpose: Default body paragraph for running text.

AbstractHeading.purpose: Heading for the Abstract / Resume section.

TABLEOFCONTENTS.purpose: Heading for the Table of Contents page.

ForewordPublishedby2ndpage.purpose: Heading on the foreword / 'published by' page.

research_document.list_marker_note: Same list markers as the advisory/response template (solid bullet / 'o' / '1.' / 'a.').

non_standard_styles.description: Styles found applied in the source templates that appear to be imported clutter or one-off artifacts rather than intended CSAS styles. They are NOT part of the spec. A conversion tool should leave them alone or remap their content to a standard style.

items.reason: Generic auto-generated name; bold Arial 11 pt character run - equivalent to Body Text + Bold.
Helvetica 9 pt character style - a non-CSAS font, almost certainly pasted in from another document.
Auto-generated name, resolves to Calibri 11 pt - paste artifact.
Italic blue underlined hyperlink variant used only by Science Advisory Reports; treat as document-specific, not a shared CSAS style.
Microsoft web/HTML paste artifact, not a CSAS style.
